q: Transition elements are the metals that exist in block between Groups
Transition elements are the metals that exist in block between Groups
_______ are elements that have partially filled d subshells (orbitals) which can give rise to cations with incomplete d subshells (orbitals).
Under IUPAC definition, groups 3 to 12 are the _______.
The _______ lanthanide and actinide series are called the inner transition elements.
There are mainly _______ series of transition elements.
The first series 3d transition elements run from _______.

The transition elements are called the _______ elements.

The first row of the transition elements is in the _______ of the Periodic Table.
In the 3d series, there are _______ transition elements.

The 4s orbital is _______ in energy than the 3d orbitals.
In atoms of the transition elements, the _______ orbital is normally filled and the rest of the electrons occupy the _______ orbitals.

The electronic configuration of 29Cu is _______.
The general essential electronic configuration of neutral atoms of the transition elements is _______.
The general essential electronic configuration of the ions of the transition elements is _______.
Which two particles have the same electronic configurations? (Given 21Sc and 22Ti)
Completely filled 3d orbitals are so stable that zinc can form compounds in the _______ oxidation state.
Which element has a high density and a high melting point in Periodic Table?
Which statements describe properties of transition elements? 1. They form coloured compounds. 2. They have variable oxidation states. 3. They have low densities. 4. They are volatile.
A characteristic property of a transition element is _______.
Which property is correct for all metals?
a: They are good conductors of electricity

q: Element M forms both M⁺ and M²⁺ ions. In which part of the Periodic Table is it placed?

a: It can act as a catalyst and some of its compounds can also act as catalysts.

It has a low density and a piece of it will float on water.
It is a very poor conductor of electricity.

q: Which property is shown by transition metals but not shown by Group I metals?
a: loss of electrons to form positive ions
